headlineSummary: HN sentiment was mostly neutral across the providers, with Microsoft Copilot getting a limited-evidence negative legal/IP jab [E5], OpenAI drawing mixed reaction around Stargate and compute-partner issues [E3][E4], and Anthropic balancing praise for Claude Code with a cost complaint [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot saw limited-evidence negativity from a comment that used it in a copyright-infringement test involving leaked source code [E5].
OpenAI was mixed-to-neutral overall: one item was factual news about changing Stargate expansion plans, while another comment criticized its repeated compute-partner problems [E3][E4].

Anthropic was mixed-to-neutral with limited evidence, because Claude Code was praised for handling Bash/Linux tools even as another comment complained that Claude burned through tokens very quickly [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code / Codex CLI / etc are all great because they know how to drive Bash and other Linux tools.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and praises it as "great" for using Bash and Linux tools.
The comment explicitly names Codex CLI and praises it as "great" for using Bash and Linux tools.

evidence.excerpt: Then I tried it with Claude - great but it blew through hundreds of dollars in tokens in about an hour.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt gives mixed sentiment: "great" praises Claude's performance, while the token spend criticism is strongly negative on cost.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI is walking away from expanding its Stargate data center with Oracle

annotations.rationale: The title directly reports an OpenAI change in data center expansion plans; it suggests a setback but is phrased as factual news rather than clear praise or condemnation.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI has had problems with every compute partner they've ever had. It's just not a solvable problem, who would they go to to allegedly get next-gen chips quicker?

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly criticizes OpenAI's history with compute partners and frames the issue as unsolved.

evidence.excerpt: Take the recently leaked Minecraft source code and have Copilot build an exact replica in another programming language and then publish it as open source. See if Microsoft believes AI is copyright infringement or not.

annotations.rationale: The comment invokes Copilot as a tool for making an "exact replica" from leaked code and frames this as a copyright-infringement test, which is critical in a legal/IP sense.
